negative_variants.description: Always Enable Saturating Counter: forces the main output to zero, so coarse compilation passes but behavioral stimulus points fail.
Always Enable Saturating Counter: uses a shifted or inverted decision condition that can pass one mode but fails boundary cases.
Always Enable Saturating Counter: mishandles reset or initialization, passing steady-state samples but failing startup or recovery checks.
Always Enable Saturating Counter: keeps the main datapath plausible while reporting the wrong metric value.
Always Enable Saturating Counter: uses a wrong gain or scale factor, leaving shape plausible while calibrated checks fail.
